era_context: Shalmaneser III's reign continues in Assyria. The Levantine states remain under Assyrian pressure. In Greece, the Archaic period is progressing; early colonization is beginning. Western Zhou in China is in its final decades. Egypt remains politically fragmented.

events.title: Shalmaneser III's continued Levantine dominance
events.description: Shalmaneser III maintained Assyrian military and tributary control over the Levant through continued campaigns. The pattern of annual military expeditions, documented in royal inscriptions, reinforced Assyrian hegemony and extracted tribute from subject states. This period represents the consolidation of Assyrian imperial power that would define the next century of Near Eastern politics.
events.certainty_note: Shalmaneser III's campaigns are well-documented; specific year assignment follows Assyrian chronology with inherent ±2 year uncertainty.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events of 817 BCE are individually attested. The year is reconstructed from the broader pattern of Shalmaneser III's reign. Chronological uncertainty in Assyrian dating systems means this year could be off by 1–2 years in some scholarly systems.
historiographic_note: 817 BCE is not individually documented in any surviving source. The year is assigned significance only through its position within Shalmaneser III's reign. This year exemplifies the problem of ancient chronology: most years leave no discrete historical trace. The surviving record is dominated by Assyrian royal inscriptions; other regions and perspectives are essentially absent.

graph_edges.note: Continuation of Shalmaneser III's sustained campaign pattern.
